nodes.name: Delete a project
nodes.description: Deletes the specified GitHub project (Projects V2) using the GraphQL API. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects V2 GraphQL API. The legacy Projects (classic) REST API was sunset on April 1, 2025 and is no longer available. The project is permanently deleted and cannot be recovered. The user must have admin permissions for the project to delete it.

nodes.name: Delete a project card (Classic - Deprecated)
nodes.description: Deletes a project card from a GitHub 'Project (classic)'; this operation is idempotent. DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Classic Projects (V1) and its REST API were sunset on April 1, 2025. This action may return a 404 error on GitHub.com. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 which uses the GraphQL API instead. See: https://github.blog/changelog/2024-05-23-sunset-notice-projects-classic/

nodes.name: Delete a project field (column)
nodes.description: Deletes a project field (column) from a GitHub Projects V2 project using the GraphQL API. IMPORTANT: GitHub Classic Projects REST API was sunset on April 1, 2025. This action now uses the GitHub Projects V2 GraphQL API with the deleteProjectV2Field mutation. Note: The built-in Status field cannot be deleted as it is a core field in GitHub Projects V2. Only custom fields can be deleted.

nodes.name: Delete project item for user
nodes.description: Tool to delete a project item for a user in GitHub Projects V2. Use when you need to remove an item from a user's project. Requires the project item's GraphQL node ID (starts with 'PVTI_'). Uses the GitHub GraphQL API deleteProjectV2Item mutation.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The GitHub username is a semantic identifier that the user naturally provides when referring to their account.

nodes.name: Delete a pull request comment reaction

nodes.name: Delete social accounts for the authenticated user

nodes.name: Delete team discussion comment reaction

requiresUserInput.rationale: Organization name is a semantic identifier provided by the user; no tool produces it.

nodes.name: Delete User List
nodes.description: Tool to delete a GitHub user list using GraphQL. Use when you need to remove a user list from a GitHub account.

nodes.name: Delete a package for a user
nodes.description: Deletes a package owned by the specified user, requiring admin permissions for the authenticated user; deletion of public packages with over 5,000 downloads may require GitHub support.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The unique name of the package is semantic content that only the user can specify (e.g., 'my-awesome-lib'). It is not an ID produced by another tool.

nodes.name: Delete workflow run logs
nodes.description: Deletes all logs for a specific workflow run in a GitHub repository, provided the repository and run exist.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name is a reference typically obtained from a prior tool that lists/creates repos or from user selection of an existing repo.
Repository owner is a reference typically obtained from a prior tool or user context identifying the account.

nodes.name: Disable a custom protection rule for an environment
nodes.description: Disables a specific, currently active custom deployment protection rule for an existing environment within a GitHub repository.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository name must be obtained from a prior tool that lists or retrieves repositories, or identified by the user; treated as a reference to a specific repo entity.
The account owner (user or organization) is a reference to an owner entity that should be resolved or provided as a known identifier.
The environment name is a semantic identifier provided by the user; it describes which environment to target and may need URL encoding but is fundamentally user-specified content.

nodes.name: Disable a workflow
nodes.description: Disables a specified workflow (by ID or filename) in a given GitHub repository, preventing new automatic triggers; any in-progress runs will continue.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name is a reference to a specific GitHub repository that typically comes from a prior list/search operation or user lookup.
Repository owner (username or organization) is a reference identifier that typically comes from repository context or prior lookup.

nodes.name: Disable private vulnerability reporting for a repository
nodes.description: Disables private vulnerability reporting for an existing GitHub repository, preventing direct private vulnerability reports to maintainers via GitHub's interface for this repository.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository name is a reference to an existing GitHub repository that must be identified, typically produced by a list/get repo tool or provided by user as a lookup target
The account owner is a reference to an existing GitHub user or organization that must be identified, typically produced by a lookup tool or known from context

nodes.name: Disable Repository Actions In Org
nodes.description: Removes a repository from the list of selected repositories enabled for GitHub Actions in an organization. Only works when the organization's `enabled_repositories` policy is set to `selected`. Requires admin:org scope.

nodes.name: Dismiss a review for a pull request
nodes.description: Dismisses an APPROVED or CHANGES_REQUESTED review on a pull request with a mandatory explanatory message. IMPORTANT: Only reviews in APPROVED or CHANGES_REQUESTED state can be dismissed. Reviews in COMMENTED, PENDING, or already DISMISSED state will return a 422 error. To dismiss a review on a protected branch, you must be a repository admin or be authorized to dismiss pull request reviews.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must be obtained from a prior tool that lists or creates repositories, or from user context
Owner (username or organization) must be obtained from context or a prior GitHub tool
The dismissal reason is semantic content only a human can naturally provide

nodes.name: Download an artifact
nodes.description: Downloads a GitHub Actions workflow artifact as a ZIP file. Returns the artifact as a downloadable file. Use GITHUB_LIST_ARTIFACTS_FOR_A_REPOSITORY or GITHUB_LIST_WORKFLOW_RUN_ARTIFACTS to get valid artifact IDs first.

nodes.name: Download a repository tarball
nodes.description: Downloads a repository's source code as a tarball (.tar.gz) archive for a specific Git reference, if the repository is accessible.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository owner is a GitHub account identifier that must be known or resolved beforehand; typically comes from a prior tool that listed or fetched repository metadata
The repository name is a GitHub resource identifier that must be known or resolved beforehand; typically comes from a prior tool that listed or fetched repositories

nodes.name: Download a repository archive ZIP
nodes.description: Downloads a repository's source code as a ZIP archive for a specific Git reference (branch, tag, or commit SHA). IMPORTANT SIZE LIMITATION: This action may fail with 'payload too large' errors for large repositories due to platform size restrictions. If you encounter size limit issues, consider these alternatives: - Use GITHUB_GET_REPOSITORY_CONTENT to fetch specific files or directories (also lists directory contents) - Clone the repository using git if you need the full codebase Best suited for: small to medium repositories, downloading specific tagged releases, or quick code inspection.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository owner username must be specified; typically comes from prior repository lookup or user knowledge of the GitHub account
The repository name must be specified; typically comes from prior repository lookup or user knowledge of the target repository
The Git reference (branch, tag, or commit SHA) is semantic content specifying which version to download; user provides this naturally as part of the download request

nodes.name: Download job logs for a workflow run
nodes.description: Downloads logs for a specific job in a GitHub Actions workflow run, contingent on the repository's existence and the job ID being valid and having produced logs.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository name is a required identifier that must match an existing repository; typically obtained from a prior tool that lists or creates repositories
The account owner (user or organization) is a required identifier that pairs with repo to uniquely identify a repository; typically obtained from a prior tool or user context

nodes.name: Download workflow run attempt logs
nodes.description: Downloads a ZIP archive of logs for a specific workflow run attempt. Returns a downloadable ZIP file containing logs for all jobs and steps in the workflow run.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must be identified; typically comes from a prior tool that listed or created repos, or user provides it by name requiring lookup
Repository owner must be identified; typically comes from a prior tool or user context

nodes.name: Download workflow run logs
nodes.description: Downloads logs for a specific GitHub Actions workflow run as a ZIP archive containing log files for each job in the workflow.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must be a real repository; typically obtained from a prior GitHub tool that lists or retrieves repositories, or user must specify it as a known repo name
Repository owner/account name must correspond to a real GitHub account; typically from a prior tool that lists repos or GitHub context

nodes.name: Enable a workflow
nodes.description: Reactivates a currently disabled GitHub Actions workflow within a repository using its workflow ID or filename.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name is a structural identifier that must be known; typically produced by a tool that lists or retrieves repositories, or provided by user as a known repository name
Owner (username or organization) is a structural identifier that must be known; typically produced by a tool that lists repositories or organizations, or provided by user

nodes.name: Enable GitHub actions in selected repositories
nodes.description: Sets the specific repositories that can use GitHub Actions within an organization by replacing the current list; only applies if the organization's Actions policy is 'selected repositories'.

nodes.name: Enable private vulnerability reporting for a repository
nodes.description: Enables private vulnerability reporting for a repository, allowing security researchers to privately submit vulnerability reports to maintainers.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository name must be known; typically obtained from a prior tool that lists or creates repositories, or user must provide it by name requiring lookup
The repository owner (username or organization) must be known; typically obtained from prior context or lookup of repository ownership

nodes.name: Enable repo for Github Actions
nodes.description: Adds a repository to the list of selected repositories enabled for GitHub Actions in an organization. Requires the organization's 'enabled_repositories' policy to be set to 'selected'.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Organization name is a semantic identifier that the user must provide; it is not derived from another tool's output.

nodes.name: Encrypt and update dev secret
nodes.description: Creates or updates a repository's development environment secret using an `encrypted_value` and its corresponding `key_id`; the secret must be pre-encrypted with the repository's Codespaces public key.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must be provided by user or produced by a tool that lists/creates repositories
Repository owner (account name) must be provided by user or produced by a tool
Name of the secret is semantic content only a human can specify

nodes.name: Encrypt org dev env secret
nodes.description: Creates or updates an organization's GitHub Codespaces secret using an encrypted value and its corresponding public key ID.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Organization name is a semantic identifier the user must specify; it's case-insensitive text input
Visibility is a semantic choice ('all' vs 'selected' vs 'private') that only the user can decide
The name of the secret to create/update is semantic content chosen by the user

nodes.name: Export a codespace for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Triggers an export of a user's specified codespace, automatically stopping it if active, and returns its export status and download URL.

nodes.name: Export an SBOM for a repository
nodes.description: Exports the software bill of materials (SBOM) in SPDX JSON format for a repository, if its dependency graph is enabled and it has at least one commit.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository name is a required identifier that typically comes from a prior tool that lists or creates repositories, or is looked up by name. It serves as a canonical reference to a specific repository.

The owner (username or organization) is a required identifier that pairs with repo to uniquely identify a repository. It is typically produced by tools that list repositories or organizations, or resolved via lookup from natural language.

nodes.name: Find Pull Requests
nodes.description: Primary tool to find and search pull requests. Supports filtering by repository, author, state, labels, and merge status, and returns structured PR data for reliable use in workflows. GitHub search results are capped at ~1000 total items; narrow filters when totals approach this limit. `created_since`/`updated_since` filter on creation/update timestamps, not `merged_at`; apply merge-date filtering client-side.

nodes.name: Find Repositories
nodes.description: AI-optimized repository search with smart filtering by language, stars, topics, and ownership. Builds intelligent search queries and returns clean, actionable repository data. Check `incomplete_results` in the response — when true, results are non-exhaustive. Search endpoints are rate-limited to ~30 requests/minute (vs. 5000/hour for general REST); apply backoff on 403/429. Total results are capped at ~1000 per query regardless of pagination.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Required search query for repository name, description, or README content; semantic content that only a user can provide

nodes.name: Follow Organization (GraphQL)
nodes.description: Tool to follow a GitHub organization using GraphQL. Use when you need to make the authenticated user follow an organization.

nodes.name: Follow a user
nodes.description: Allows the authenticated user to follow the GitHub user specified by `username`; this action is idempotent and the user cannot follow themselves.

nodes.name: Follow User (GraphQL)
nodes.description: Tool to follow a GitHub user using GraphQL. Use when you need to make the authenticated user follow another user.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The userId must be a valid GitHub Node ID (format 'MDQ6VXNlcjU4MzIzMQ=='), which is an opaque identifier that another tool must produce or a user lookup must resolve. Cannot be inferred from natural language username alone.

nodes.name: Force cancel a workflow run
nodes.description: Forcefully cancels a queued or in-progress GitHub Actions workflow run, bypassing conditions like always() that would otherwise continue execution. Only use when the standard cancel endpoint fails. Cannot cancel completed workflow runs. Requires write permissions to the repository.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must be obtained from a prior tool that lists or retrieves repositories, or user must provide it explicitly as a known repository identifier
Repository owner (account name) must come from a tool that fetches repository metadata or be known from prior GitHub operations

nodes.name: Fork a gist
nodes.description: Forks a specified public gist, creating a copy under the authenticated user's account.

nodes.name: Generate release notes content for a release
nodes.description: Generates Markdown release notes content (listing changes, pull requests, and contributors) for a GitHub repository release, customizable via tags and a configuration file.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The GitHub repository owner (username or organization); must be known or retrieved from a prior tool that identified the repository
The GitHub repository name; must be known or retrieved from a prior tool that identified the repository
The tag name for the release; typically provided by the user as a semantic version or release identifier (e.g., 'v1.0.0')

nodes.name: Get a blob
nodes.description: Retrieves the raw, typically Base64-encoded, content of a file (blob) from a GitHub repository using its SHA hash, if the repository and blob SHA exist.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The owner (username or organization) is a GitHub identity that typically comes from a prior lookup or search tool that identified the repository owner
The repository name is typically obtained from a prior tool that listed or searched repositories

nodes.name: Get a branch
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specified branch within a GitHub repository.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository owner is a producer_ref that typically comes from a prior tool that lists or retrieves repositories, or from user identifying a GitHub account
The repository name is a producer_ref that typically comes from a prior tool that lists or creates repositories

The branch name is semantic content provided by the user; it is a string identifier but not an ID that another tool produces. Users naturally specify branch names like 'main', 'develop', or 'feature/xyz'

nodes.name: Get access restrictions
nodes.description: Lists users, teams, and GitHub Apps with push access to a branch; this branch must be protected in repository settings for detailed restrictions, otherwise expect a 404 or empty response.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name is a required identifier that must come from a prior tool that listed/created repositories or was provided by user as a concrete repo name
Repository owner (username or org) is a required identifier that typically comes from prior context or must be known to identify the repo
Branch name is a semantic string provided by the user identifying which branch's access restrictions to query

nodes.name: Get a commit
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific commit from a repository by its owner, name, and a valid commit reference (SHA, branch, or tag), supporting pagination for large diffs.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository owner must be provided; typically comes from a prior tool that listed/searched repositories or from user context
Repository name must be provided; typically comes from a prior tool that listed/searched repositories or from user context

nodes.name: Get admin branch protection
nodes.description: Checks if repository administrators are subject to the branch protection rules on a specific branch.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The owner is a GitHub account identifier that typically comes from a prior tool that lists/creates repositories or resolves a user name to their account.
The repository name is a GitHub repository identifier that typically comes from a prior tool that lists/creates repositories or searches for repos.

The branch name is a semantic identifier provided by the user as a string (e.g., 'main', 'develop'). While it could theoretically come from a prior tool that lists branches, it is commonly user-provided natural language input.

nodes.name: Get all API versions
nodes.description: Retrieves all officially supported, date-based (e.g., "2022-11-28") versions of the GitHub REST API from the /versions endpoint.

nodes.name: Get all contributor commit activity
nodes.description: Retrieves commit activity (total commits, weekly additions/deletions/commits) for all contributors to a repository; may require a retry if GitHub returns 202 while preparing data.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must be known; typically produced by a prior tool that listed/fetched repos, or user provides it directly as a known entity
Repository owner (username) must be known; typically produced by a prior tool or user provides it directly as a known entity

nodes.name: Get all deployment protection rules for an environment
nodes.description: Lists all enabled custom deployment protection rules for a specific environment in a repository; the environment must exist and be configured for deployments.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must come from a prior tool that created or listed repositories, or user must provide it as a known repository name
Owner/organization name must come from a prior tool that identified the repository owner, or user must provide it as a known owner name

nodes.name: Get a milestone
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific milestone within a GitHub repository by its number.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must be provided, typically from a prior tool that listed or created repositories, or user must specify it directly as a known repo name
Repository owner (username) must be provided, typically from a prior tool that identified the repo owner, or user must specify it directly as a known GitHub username

nodes.name: Get an app
nodes.description: Retrieves publicly available information for an existing GitHub App, identified by its unique URL-friendly `app_slug`.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The app slug is a semantic identifier that only a user can provide by naming the GitHub App they want to look up. It is a human-readable, URL-friendly string that does not come from another tool's output.

nodes.name: Get an artifact
nodes.description: Gets a specific artifact for a repository by `artifact_id`. This action retrieves the details of a specific artifact from a GitHub repository using the artifact's unique identifier. It allows users to access information about artifacts stored in the repository.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository name is a required identifier that must be known; typically obtained from a prior tool that lists or creates repositories
The owner/organization name is a required identifier that must be known; typically obtained from a prior tool or user context

nodes.name: Get an assignment
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific GitHub Classroom assignment if the authenticated user is an administrator of the classroom.

nodes.name: Get an autolink reference of a repository
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific autolink reference (which automatically hyperlinks text like 'JIRA-123' to an external system) for a repository using its unique ID; requires administrator access to the repository.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name is a required identifier that typically comes from a prior tool that lists or creates repositories, or from user knowledge of an existing repo
Owner is a required identifier (username or organization) that must be known or produced by a prior operation

nodes.name: Get an environment
nodes.description: Retrieves the details of a specific deployment environment for a given repository, including its name, configurations, and current status.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name is a required identifier that must be known; typically produced by a tool that lists or creates repositories, or provided by user as a known repo name
Repository owner (username) is a required identifier; typically produced by GitHub tools or provided by user as a known account name
The name of the deployment environment is semantic content that must be specified by the user; the tool description indicates it must be URL-encoded but the actual name is user-provided

nodes.name: Get an environment public key
nodes.description: Retrieves the public key for a specified GitHub repository environment, used to encrypt secrets for GitHub Actions.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The owner is typically produced by repository lookup or list tools; can be user-provided if user knows exact owner name
The repo name is typically produced by repository lookup or list tools; can be user-provided if user knows exact repo name
Environment name must be specified by the user and is semantic content; it requires URL encoding but the encoding is a technical transformation, not a lookup

nodes.name: Get an environment secret
nodes.description: Retrieves metadata (name and timestamps) for a single secret in a GitHub Actions environment. Note: The actual secret value is never returned by this API for security reasons.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must be obtained from a prior tool that lists/creates/fetches repositories, or user provides the exact repo name they own
Owner username/organization must be resolved from a prior tool or provided by user as their own login
The name of the secret is semantic content the user must specify; it identifies which secret to retrieve
The environment name is semantic content the user must provide to identify which GitHub Actions environment contains the secret

nodes.name: Get an environment variable
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific environment variable from a GitHub Actions environment by repository owner, repository name, environment name, and variable name.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The name of the environment variable is semantic content only a user can specify; it's not an ID that another tool produces
Repository name is a canonical identifier that must be provided by the user or produced by a tool that lists/creates repositories
Repository owner (username or organization) is a canonical identifier that must be provided by the user or produced by a tool
The name of the GitHub Actions environment is semantic content the user must specify; while it may need URL-encoding, it is not an ID produced by another tool

nodes.name: Get an issue
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific issue in a repository using the owner, repository name, and issue number.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name is typically produced by a prior tool that lists or searches repositories, or must reference a specific repo the user knows
Repository owner is typically produced by a prior tool, or must reference a specific GitHub username

nodes.name: Get an issue comment
nodes.description: Action to get an issue comment.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository owner must be identified; typically comes from a prior tool that listed or created a repository, or from user specifying an org/user name that may need lookup
The repository name must be identified; typically comes from a prior tool that listed repositories or created one

nodes.name: Get an issue event
nodes.description: Fetches details of a specific issue event for a given repository and event ID.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The owner username must come from a prior tool that lists/gets repositories or from user specifying an exact owner name; typically resolved via repository lookup or user input
The repository name must come from a prior tool that lists repositories or from user specifying an exact repo name; paired with owner to identify a unique repository

nodes.name: Get an organization
nodes.description: Get an organization

requiresUserInput.rationale: The organization name is a natural language identifier that the user provides; it is not case-sensitive and represents semantic knowledge about which organization to fetch

nodes.name: Get an organization public key
nodes.description: Action for `GetAnOrganizationPublicKey`.

nodes.name: Get an organization role
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific GitHub organization role by its ID.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The organization name is semantic content that only the user can provide. While it could theoretically be a lookup_required scenario, the parameter expects a direct organization name string, not a reference to another tool's output.

nodes.name: Get an organization secret
nodes.description: Gets an organization secret's metadata (e.g., name, creation/update dates, visibility), but not its encrypted value.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The secret name is semantic content identifying which specific secret metadata to retrieve

nodes.name: Get an organization variable
nodes.description: Retrieves details (name, value, visibility, timestamps) of a specific, existing variable for an existing GitHub organization.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The organization name is a semantic identifier that the user must provide; it is not an ID produced by another tool
The variable name is semantic content that the user must provide to identify which specific organization variable to retrieve

nodes.name: Get an organization webhook
nodes.description: Retrieves the full configuration, including subscribed events and delivery settings, for an existing organization webhook.

nodes.name: Get a package for an organization
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific package (by type and name) from an organization, if both the package and organization exist.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The semantic name of the package is domain knowledge only a user can provide; it is a search/filter term
The package type (maven, container, docker) is a categorical choice that only the user knows based on their package infrastructure

nodes.name: Get a package for a user
nodes.description: Retrieves metadata for a specific package owned by a GitHub user, using package type, name, and username as identifiers.

requiresUserInput.rationale: GitHub username must be provided by the user or looked up from context; it is semantic content identifying a person
Package name is a semantic identifier that the user specifies; it is not produced by another tool in a typical workflow
Package type is a categorical choice (maven, container, docker) that the user must specify based on their knowledge of the package

nodes.name: Get a package for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific package owned by the authenticated user.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The package name is semantic content that only a user can specify; it's a natural language identifier of which package to retrieve
The package type is a categorical choice (maven, container, or docker) that the user must provide based on their registry type

nodes.name: Get a package version for an organization
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific version of a package within an organization.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Package name is a semantic identifier provided by the user to specify which package to query
Package type is a categorical choice (maven, container, docker) that user must specify or know

nodes.name: Get a package version for a user
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific public package version associated with a GitHub user.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Package name is semantic content that only the user can specify
Package type is a categorical choice (maven, container, docker) that only the user can specify

nodes.name: Get authenticated user package version
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for an existing specific package version associated with the authenticated user, identified by its type, name, and version ID.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The package name is semantic content that only the user can specify; it's not derived from another tool's output
The package type is a categorical choice (maven, container, docker, npm, etc.) that the user must specify based on their package registry

nodes.name: Get a project
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific GitHub project (V2) using its project number and owner. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects V2 REST API. The legacy Projects (classic) REST API was sunset on April 1, 2025. The project is identified by its number (visible in the project URL) and owner (either a username or organization name). If no owner is specified, the authenticated user is assumed.

nodes.name: Get a project card
nodes.description: Retrieves all details of a specific project card, given its unique `card_id`. DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Projects (classic) REST API was sunset on April 1, 2025. This action only works with existing classic project cards that were created before the sunset date. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 (GraphQL API) instead.

nodes.name: Get a project column (Classic - Deprecated)
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific project column from GitHub Projects (classic). DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Classic Projects (V1) and its REST API were sunset on April 1, 2025. This action will return 404 for most requests on GitHub.com. However, it may still work on GitHub Enterprise Server instances where classic projects are enabled. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 which uses the GraphQL API instead. See: https://github.blog/changelog/2024-05-23-sunset-notice-projects-classic/ Returns column details including: id, name, url, project_url, cards_url, node_id, created_at, and updated_at timestamps.

nodes.name: Get a pull request
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific pull request from a GitHub repository using its owner, repository name, and pull request number.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository owner username must come from a prior tool that lists/retrieves repositories or user context, or be user-provided; typically a producer_ref in multi-step workflows
Repository name must come from a prior tool that lists/searches repositories, or be user-provided; typically a producer_ref in multi-step workflows

nodes.name: Get a reference
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific Git reference (e.g., a branch, tag, or fully qualified like 'heads/main') from a GitHub repository. The reference must exist in the repository; use 'list_matching_references' first if unsure what references are available.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The Git reference format (branch, tag, or pull request reference) is semantic content specifying which ref to retrieve. While it could theoretically come from a list tool, the description explicitly suggests using 'list_matching_references' only if unsure, implying the user typically provides this directly.
The repository name must be obtained from a prior tool that lists or creates repositories, or can be user-provided as a known repository identifier.
The owner (username or organization) must be obtained from a prior tool or user context; it is a structured identifier rather than free-form content.

nodes.name: Get a release
nodes.description: Gets a specific release from a GitHub repository, provided the repository is accessible and the release exists.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must come from a prior tool that lists or retrieves repositories, or be explicitly named by the user as a known identifier
Repository owner (GitHub username) must come from a prior tool or be explicitly provided; typically paired with repo in workflows

nodes.name: Get a release asset
nodes.description: Gets metadata for a specific release asset in a GitHub repository, including a `browser_download_url` for downloading the asset.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must be provided by a prior tool that lists or creates repositories, or by user who knows the exact repo name
Repository owner (username or org) must be provided by a prior tool or user knowledge

nodes.name: Get a release by tag name
nodes.description: Gets a release from a GitHub repository by its tag name; the repository and a release with this tag must already exist.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The tag name is a semantic identifier the user must provide or know in advance; it cannot be derived from another tool's output without explicit search.
The repository name is typically produced by tools that list or get repositories, or can come from parsing output of prior GitHub operations.
The owner/account is typically produced by prior GitHub operations or repository lookups, or provided as a constant if working within a known organization.

nodes.name: Get a repository
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information about an existing and accessible GitHub repository.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must be provided or retrieved from a prior tool that produced repo references
Repository owner username must be provided or retrieved from a prior tool that produced owner references

nodes.name: Get a repository public key
nodes.description: Gets a repository's public key for encrypting secrets to be used in GitHub Actions, if the repository exists and is accessible.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository owner (account/organization) must be known; typically a producer_ref from a prior tool that listed repos or was explicitly named by the user as a known entity
The repository name must be known; typically a producer_ref from a prior tool that listed repos or was explicitly identified by the user

nodes.name: Get a repository readme
nodes.description: Fetches the README file (if it exists and is accessible) from a specified GitHub repository, returning its Base64-encoded content and metadata.

requiresUserInput.rationale: GitHub repository owner must be specified; typically comes from a prior search/list tool or user-provided repository context
GitHub repository name must be specified; typically comes from a prior search/list tool or user-provided repository context

nodes.name: Get a repository readme for a directory
nodes.description: Retrieves the README file from a specified directory within a GitHub repository, optionally at a given commit, branch, or tag.

requiresUserInput.rationale: GitHub repository owner username, typically produced by a search/list tool or provided by user lookup
GitHub repository name, typically produced by a search/list tool or provided by user lookup
Directory path is semantic content that only a human can naturally specify (e.g., 'src/', 'docs/api/')

nodes.name: Get a repository ruleset
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific repository ruleset by its ID; if `includes_parents` is true, the search for this `ruleset_id` also extends to rulesets from parent organizations.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must be obtained from a tool that lists or creates repositories, or user must specify it by exact name
Account owner (username or organization) must be obtained from a tool that lists repositories or organizations, or user must specify it by exact name

nodes.name: Get a repository rule suite
nodes.description: Gets detailed information for a specific repository rule suite by its ID, including its evaluation status and the results of its individual rules.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must be known; typically produced by repository listing/search tools or provided by user reference to a specific repo
Owner name must be known; typically produced by GitHub listing tools or provided as a known organization/user reference

nodes.name: Get a repository secret
nodes.description: Gets metadata (name, creation/update timestamps) for an existing repository secret, excluding its encrypted value.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The owner is a GitHub username or organization that must be known or retrieved; typically comes from a prior GitHub operation or user context
The repository name must be known; typically comes from a prior GitHub operation or is paired with owner as a repository reference
The name of the specific secret to retrieve is semantic content that only a user can naturally specify

nodes.name: Get a repository subscription
nodes.description: Gets the authenticated user's subscription details for a repository, indicating if they receive notifications.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository owner's username must be supplied; in multi-step workflows this is typically resolved via a search or prior tool call that yields an owner entity. Can also be user-provided if known.
The repository name must be supplied; in multi-step workflows this is typically resolved via a search or prior tool call that yields a repo entity. Can also be user-provided if known.

nodes.name: Get a repository variable
nodes.description: Gets a specific GitHub Actions variable by name from an accessible repository.

nodes.description: Tool to fetch raw file content from a GitHub repository. Ensure owner, repo, ref, and path are consistent when combining with other GitHub tools to avoid mismatched-ref errors.

requiresUserInput.rationale: GitHub username or organization name that owns the repository. Must be known from a prior tool result or user input, typically from a GitHub search/list operation or explicit user specification.
Repository name. Must be identified from a prior GitHub operation or provided explicitly by user. Often paired with owner from a search result.

File path within the repository relative to root. User must specify the exact path they wish to retrieve. Description recommends using GITHUB_GET_A_TREE to discover valid paths, but the path itself is a user semantic choice.

nodes.name: Get allowed actions and workflows for a repository
nodes.description: Gets the settings for allowed actions and reusable workflows in a repository. Requires the repository's `allowed_actions` policy to be set to `selected` (returns 409 if set to `all` or `local_only`).

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository owner must be known; typically obtained from a prior tool that listed/created a repository or provided by user as a GitHub username/org name that may need lookup.
The repository name is typically obtained from a prior tool that listed/created repositories or is a known reference in the workflow.

nodes.name: Get repository development environment secret
nodes.description: Gets metadata (name, creation/update timestamps) for a specific, existing development environment secret (Codespaces secret) in a repository, without exposing its value.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The username or organization that owns the repository must be known; typically produced by a repo lookup or provided by user as a known identifier
The repository name is a required reference; typically produced by a repo lookup or list tool, or provided by user as a known identifier

nodes.name: Get repository attestations
nodes.description: Tool to retrieve attestations by subject digest from a GitHub repository. Use when you need to verify or check attestations for a specific artifact identified by its SHA256 digest.

requiresUserInput.rationale: SHA256 digest value is semantic content that must be provided by the user or extracted from a build/artifact context

nodes.name: Get repository clones
nodes.description: Retrieves the total number of clones and a breakdown of clone activity (daily or weekly) for a specified repository over the preceding 14 days.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The owner username must be known; typically produced by a repository lookup or provided directly by user as a GitHub handle
The repository name must be known; typically produced by a repository search/list tool or provided by the user

nodes.name: Get repository content
nodes.description: Retrieves a file's Base64 encoded content or lists a directory's contents from a GitHub repository.

requiresUserInput.rationale: GitHub repository owner must be provided or resolved; can come from a prior tool that lists repositories or from user input naming an org/user
Repository name is a required identifier; typically produced by a search/list tool or provided by user, but when needed as a dependency, comes from another tool's output
File or directory path within the repository is semantic content the user specifies (e.g., 'src/main.js', 'README.md', or '' for root)

nodes.name: Get Repository via GraphQL
nodes.description: Tool to lookup a repository by owner and name using GitHub's GraphQL API. Use when you need comprehensive repository information.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository name is semantic content that must come from the user or a prior context describing which repo to look up
The owner login (user or organization name) is semantic content the user must provide or be available from context

nodes.name: Get Repository Owner
nodes.description: Tool to lookup a repository owner (User or Organization) by login using GitHub's GraphQL API. Use when you need to determine whether a login is a user or organization and retrieve basic profile information.

nodes.name: Get repository permissions for a user
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific user's permission level ('admin', 'write', 'read', or 'none') for a given repository, where 'maintain' role is reported as 'write' and 'triage' as 'read'.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository owner is typically a GitHub account identifier that comes from a prior tool result (e.g., from listing repositories or getting repository details). While it could be user-provided, in agent workflows it's commonly a producer reference.

The repository name is typically obtained from a prior tool result (e.g., from listing or searching repositories). In agent workflows, it's a reference to a repository entity produced by another tool.

nodes.name: Retrieve repo public key for encryption
nodes.description: Gets a repository's public key, used to encrypt secrets for Dependabot.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository owner (account name) must be provided or resolved from a prior tool that lists/retrieves repository details
The repository name must be provided or resolved from a prior tool that lists/retrieves repository details

nodes.name: Get repository secret securely
nodes.description: Retrieves metadata for an existing Dependabot secret in a repository, without exposing its encrypted value.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The username or organization that owns the repository must be known; typically produced by a repository lookup or provided by user knowledge of their own org/username
The repository name must be known; typically produced by a repository lookup or list tool, or user-provided if they know their own repo
The name of the specific Dependabot secret to retrieve is semantic content that only the user can specify

nodes.name: Get repository security advisory
nodes.description: Tool to retrieve a repository security advisory using its GHSA identifier. Use when you need detailed information about a specific security vulnerability.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must be provided, typically from a prior tool that listed or created repositories, or user-specified directly
Repository owner must be provided, typically from a prior tool that listed or created repositories, or user-specified directly
GHSA identifier is a specific security advisory identifier that only the user can provide when they want to retrieve details about a known vulnerability

nodes.name: Get a delivery for a repository webhook
nodes.description: Retrieves a specific delivery for a repository webhook, identified by its `hook_id` and `delivery_id`.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository name must be known or produced by a prior tool that lists/searches repositories
The repository owner must be known or produced by a prior tool that identifies the account owner

nodes.name: Get rules for a branch
nodes.description: Retrieves all active rules for a specific branch in a GitHub repository, excluding rules in 'evaluate' or 'disabled' status.

requiresUserInput.rationale: GitHub repository owner must be specified; typically comes from a prior tool that identified or listed a repository
Repository name is required and typically comes from a tool that searched for or listed repositories
Branch name is a semantic identifier that the user specifies; it is literal branch reference, not a resolvable lookup

nodes.name: Get Organization Dependabot Secret
nodes.description: Gets a single organization Dependabot secret's metadata (name, timestamps, visibility) without revealing its encrypted value. Requires admin:org scope.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The secret name is a semantic identifier provided by the user to specify which Dependabot secret to retrieve

nodes.name: Get status checks protection
nodes.description: Retrieves the status check protection settings for a specific branch in a GitHub repository, if status check protection is enabled for it.

requiresUserInput.rationale: GitHub account owner identifier, typically produced by a repository lookup or provided as a known organization/user name
Repository name, typically produced by a repository search/list tool or known in advance
Branch name is a semantic identifier that must be provided by the user; no wildcard support means it's a specific literal branch name

nodes.name: Get team membership for a user
nodes.description: Retrieves a user's role and membership status within a specific team in an organization. Returns membership details including role ('member' or 'maintainer') and state ('active' or 'pending'). If the user is not a member of the team, returns is_member=False with a descriptive message. Requires the authenticated user to be an organization member with visibility into the team.

nodes.name: Get teams with access to the protected branch
nodes.description: Lists teams with explicit push access to a protected branch, provided team restrictions are configured in the branch's protection settings; returns an empty list otherwise.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must be obtained from a prior tool that lists or creates repositories, or user provides it as a known repository identifier
Repository owner (username) must be obtained from a prior tool or user provides it as a known GitHub user/organization identifier
Branch name is semantic content specifying which protected branch to query; user naturally provides this as a branch reference

nodes.name: Get the authenticated user
nodes.description: Gets the profile information for the currently authenticated GitHub user, including potentially private details based on user settings.

nodes.name: Get the combined status for a specific reference
nodes.description: Retrieves the aggregated commit status (e.g., success, failure, pending) from all checks for a specific reference (SHA, branch, or tag) in a GitHub repository.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository owner (username or organization) must be provided or resolved; typically comes from a previous tool that listed repositories or from user input identifying the owner
The repository name is a producer_ref that typically comes from a search/list tool or prior context; it identifies the specific repo being queried

nodes.name: Get the hourly commit count for each day
nodes.description: Retrieves the 'punch card' data, showing hourly commit counts for each day of the week for an existing and accessible repository.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must be identified; typically comes from a prior list/search or user names it. Agent workflows need repo as a concrete reference to a repository that was discovered or created.
Owner (username or organization name) is a required identifier paired with repo. Typically resolved from prior context, repo discovery, or user specification of a known owner.

nodes.name: Get the last year of commit activity
nodes.description: Fetches weekly commit totals and daily commit counts for the last 52 weeks for a specified GitHub repository.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository name is a canonical identifier that must be known; typically produced by a repo search/list tool or provided by the user as a known repo identifier
The owner (username or organization) is a canonical identifier that must be known; typically produced by a user/org lookup tool or provided by the user as a known identifier

nodes.name: Get the latest release
nodes.description: Fetches the latest official (non-prerelease, non-draft) release for a GitHub repository; requires at least one such published release.

requiresUserInput.rationale: GitHub repository owner must be provided; typically a username or organization name that identifies the account owning the repo
GitHub repository name must be provided; identifies the specific repository from which to fetch the latest release

nodes.name: Get the license for a repository
nodes.description: Retrieves the license file and its details for a repository, optionally from a specific Git reference (branch, tag, or commit SHA).

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository owner (username or organization) must be provided; typically obtained from a prior GitHub tool that returns repository details or user context
The repository name must be provided; typically obtained from a prior GitHub tool that returns repository details or a search result

nodes.name: Get the review history for a workflow run
nodes.description: Retrieves the detailed approval history for a specific workflow run in a GitHub repository, detailing each review's environment, state, reviewer, and comments, to track the approval process for workflows, particularly automated deployments.

requiresUserInput.rationale: GitHub repository owner must be known; typically produced by repo search/list or provided as context from prior GitHub operation
GitHub repository name must be known; typically produced by repo search/list or provided as context from prior GitHub operation

nodes.name: Get the status of a GitHub Pages deployment
nodes.description: Retrieves the status of a specific GitHub Pages deployment for a repository, which must have GitHub Pages enabled.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must be obtained from a prior tool that lists or creates repositories, or is a known identifier in the workflow
Repository owner (username or organization) must be obtained from a prior tool or be a known identifier

nodes.name: Get the weekly commit activity
nodes.description: Fetches the weekly commit activity (additions and deletions per week) for a repository over the past year; best for repositories with under 10,000 commits.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must be provided; typically obtained from a prior tool that lists or searches repositories, or user specifies it by name
Repository owner (user or organization) must be provided; typically obtained from a prior tool or user specifies it by account name

nodes.name: Get the weekly commit count
nodes.description: Retrieves the weekly commit count for a repository, detailing commits by the owner and all contributors over the last 52 weeks; GitHub may return a 202 status or an empty response if statistics are being computed.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository owner is a required identifier that must be provided by the user or produced by a prior GitHub tool that lists/retrieves repositories
The repository name is a required identifier that must be provided by the user or produced by a prior GitHub tool that lists/retrieves repositories

nodes.name: Get the Zen of GitHub
nodes.description: Retrieves a random quote from GitHub's 'Zen of GitHub' collection, reflecting GitHub's design philosophies and offering humorous insights, useful for displaying GitHub wisdom or a lighthearted message.

nodes.name: Get top referral paths
nodes.description: Fetches the top 10 most viewed content paths for a repository from the last 14 days.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name is a required identifier that typically comes from a tool that lists or creates repositories
Owner/organization name is a required identifier that must be known or resolved beforehand

nodes.name: Get top referral sources
nodes.description: Fetches the top 10 websites that referred traffic to a repository within the last 14 days.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository owner is a reference that identifies who owns the repo; typically comes from a prior tool call or user knowledge, but agent workflows often resolve this via lookup
The repository name is a reference that must be known or produced by a prior operation; agent needs this to target the correct repo

nodes.name: Get user by ID
nodes.description: Retrieves a GitHub user's profile information by their unique numeric account ID. Use when you know the user's numeric ID rather than their username.

requiresUserInput.rationale: A numeric GitHub user ID must be obtained from another tool that produces user_id (e.g., a user search or a tool that lists users), or provided directly by the user who knows the numeric ID

nodes.name: Get user project item
nodes.description: Tool to get an item from a user-owned project in GitHub Projects V2. Use when you need to retrieve details about a specific item (issue, pull request, or draft issue) in a user's project board.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The GitHub username is semantic user input; it identifies which user's project to query

nodes.name: Get user attestations by subject digest
nodes.description: Tool to get attestations by subject digest for a GitHub user. Use when you need to retrieve attestation data for a specific artifact or resource identified by its subject digest.

requiresUserInput.rationale: GitHub username must be a valid user account, typically obtained from a prior GitHub user lookup or search
The subject digest is a specific artifact identifier (sha256: or sha512: prefixed string) that the user provides to query attestations for

nodes.name: Get user's projects v2
nodes.description: Tool to list GitHub Projects v2 for a specified user. Use when you need to retrieve all projects owned by a user with pagination support.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The GitHub username is semantic content that only a user can provide; it identifies whose projects to retrieve but is not an ID produced by another tool.

nodes.name: Get users with access to the protected branch
nodes.description: Lists users with explicit push access to a protected branch, provided its protection rule restricts pushes to specific users.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository owner (user or organization) must be provided; typically resolved from a prior tool that listed or created a repository.
The repository name must be provided; typically resolved from a prior tool that listed or created a repository.
The protected branch name is semantic content that only a human can naturally specify (e.g., 'main', 'develop').

nodes.name: Get Viewer via GraphQL
nodes.description: Tool to retrieve the authenticated user's profile information via GitHub GraphQL. Use when working with GraphQL queries or need viewer details in GraphQL format.

nodes.name: Get workflow external access level
nodes.description: Gets the access level settings for a private repository, determining how workflows outside this repository can use its actions and reusable workflows.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name is a required identifier that identifies which repository's workflow access settings to retrieve
Owner is a required identifier that, paired with repo, uniquely identifies the repository

nodes.name: Get a job for a workflow run
nodes.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific job within a GitHub Actions workflow run, given its `job_id` which must be valid for the specified repository's workflow.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository owner must be known; typically produced by a prior tool that lists/retrieves repository metadata or provided by user as a known GitHub username/org.
The repository name must be known; typically produced by a prior tool or provided by user as a known repo name.

nodes.name: Get workflow run usage
nodes.description: Gets the billable time, in milliseconds, for a specific workflow run, detailing time spent on various operating systems.

requiresUserInput.rationale: GitHub repository owner (username or organization) must be provided; typically resolved from a prior tool that listed or created a repository, or user-specified by name requiring lookup
GitHub repository name must be provided; typically resolved from a prior tool that listed repositories, or user-specified by name requiring lookup

nodes.name: Get workflow usage
nodes.description: Gets the billable time (in milliseconds, broken down by runner OS) for a specific workflow within a repository for the current billing cycle.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The owner is typically resolved from a prior tool that lists or retrieves repositories, or can be user-provided if they know the exact owner name. In agent workflows, this is usually a producer reference from a repository lookup.
The repository name is typically produced by a tool that lists or searches repositories. While technically a string, in agent workflows it's resolved as a reference from prior repository operations.

nodes.name: Check if repo starred by auth user
nodes.description: Use to determine if the authenticated user has starred a specific GitHub repository, which is confirmed by an HTTP 204 status (resulting in an empty dictionary in the response data); the action fails (e.g., HTTP 404) if the repository is not starred or does not exist.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository name is a canonical identifier that must be obtained from a prior tool that lists or retrieves GitHub repositories (e.g., GITHUB_LIST_USER_REPOS or GITHUB_SEARCH_REPOSITORIES).
The repository owner (user or organization) is a canonical identifier that must be obtained from a prior tool that retrieves repository details or user/org information.

nodes.name: List accepted assignments for an assignment
nodes.description: Lists accepted assignments (student repositories created after acceptance) for an existing GitHub Classroom assignment, identified by its unique `assignment_id`.

nodes.name: List repositories accessible via GitHub App installation
nodes.description: Lists repositories that the authenticated user can access through a specific GitHub App installation. Returns repositories where the user has explicit :read, :write, or :admin permission through the installation. The response includes permission details for each repository. Prerequisites: - Requires a GitHub App user access token (not a standard OAuth token) - Use GITHUB_LIST_APP_INSTALLATIONS first to get valid installation IDs

nodes.name: List app installations for user token

nodes.name: Get apps with access to the protected branch

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must be provided by user or sourced from a prior tool that lists/creates repositories
Repository owner (username) must be provided by user or sourced from a prior tool that identifies the owner
Protected branch name is a semantic identifier provided by the user; typically known in the context of repository branch protection rules

nodes.name: List artifacts for a repository

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository owner must be known or provided by a prior tool that resolved a GitHub organization/user; typically comes from a repository lookup or is user-provided.
The repository name must be known or provided by a prior tool; typically comes from a repository search/lookup.

nodes.name: List assignees

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository owner username must be provided, typically from a prior tool that identified the repository or user context
Repository name must be provided, typically from a prior tool that identified or listed repositories

nodes.name: List assignments for a classroom

nodes.name: List attestation repositories
nodes.description: Tool to list attestation repositories for an organization. Use when you need to discover which repositories in an organization have attestations. Attestations provide verifiable metadata about software artifacts.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Organization name is a semantic identifier provided by the user; it is the target organization to query for attestation repositories

nodes.name: List available machine types for a repository
nodes.description: Lists machine types available for GitHub Codespaces in a repository, optionally using a Git ref to check compatibility based on prebuild availability and devcontainer configurations.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Required identifier for the repository owner (username or organization name); typically produced by GitHub list/search tools or user lookup
Required identifier for the repository name; typically produced by GitHub list/search tools or derived from repository operations

nodes.name: List branches
nodes.description: Lists branches for an existing GitHub repository, with an option to filter by protection status.

requiresUserInput.rationale: GitHub account owner username must be provided or resolved from a prior tool that produced owner identity
Repository name must be provided or resolved from a prior tool that produced repo identity

nodes.name: List branches for head commit
nodes.description: Lists branches in an accessible repository where the provided commit SHA is the head, useful for identifying development lines based on that commit.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository owner must be specified; typically comes from a prior tool that identified or listed a repository
The repository name must be specified; typically comes from a prior tool that identified or listed a repository

nodes.name: List check run annotations
nodes.description: Lists annotations for a specific check run in a GitHub repository, detailing issues like errors or warnings on particular code lines.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The account owner must be provided; typically resolved from a prior GitHub operation or user context
The repository name must be provided; typically resolved from a prior GitHub operation or user context

nodes.name: List check runs for a git ref

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name is typically a producer_ref from search/list tools or a constant from prior context
Owner (username or organization) is typically a producer_ref from prior context or lookup

nodes.name: List check runs in a check suite
nodes.description: Lists check runs for a specific check suite in a GitHub repository, optionally filtering by check name or status.

nodes.name: List check suites for a git reference
nodes.description: Lists check suites for a Git reference (commit SHA, branch, or tag) in a repository, optionally filtering by GitHub App ID or check run name.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository owner must be provided or resolved from a prior tool that identified the repository or organization
The repository name must be provided or resolved from a prior tool that identified the repository

nodes.name: List child teams

requiresUserInput.rationale: Organization name is semantic content provided by the user; it is not an ID reference from another tool

nodes.name: List classrooms
nodes.description: Lists GitHub Classrooms to which the authenticated user has administrative access.

nodes.name: List codeowners errors
nodes.description: Lists syntax errors in a repository's CODEOWNERS file, which must be located at the root, `.github/`, or `docs/` directory for the specified ref.

requiresUserInput.rationale: GitHub repository owner username; typically produced by tools that list/get repositories or provided by user as part of owner+repo pair
GitHub repository name; typically produced by tools that list/get repositories or provided by user as part of owner+repo pair

nodes.name: List CodeQL databases for a repository
nodes.description: Lists all CodeQL databases for a repository where CodeQL analysis has been previously run and completed.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must be provided or obtained from a tool that produces repo identifiers
Repository owner (username) must be provided or obtained from a tool that produces owner identifiers

nodes.name: List code scanning alerts for an organization

requiresUserInput.rationale: The organization name is semantic content the user must specify; while it's a GitHub entity, it's a natural language identifier that acts as a filter parameter

nodes.name: List code scanning alerts for a repository

requiresUserInput.rationale: The account owner of the repository must be provided; typically resolved from a previous tool that produced owner identity or provided by user as a known GitHub username/org
The repository name must be provided; typically resolved from a previous tool that produced repo identity or provided by user as a known repository name

nodes.name: List code scanning analyses for a repository
nodes.description: Lists code scanning analyses for an existing repository, optionally filtering by tool (name or GUID), Git reference, or SARIF ID.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository owner is a required identifier that must be known or obtained from prior tool output
Repository name is a required identifier that must be known or obtained from prior tool output

nodes.name: Get all codes of conduct
nodes.description: Retrieves all available codes of conduct from GitHub, often used to select one for a repository.

nodes.name: List Codes of Conduct (GraphQL)
nodes.description: Tool to fetch all available codes of conduct using GitHub's GraphQL API. Use when you need to retrieve the complete list of codes of conduct that can be applied to repositories.

nodes.name: List codespaces for a user in organization

nodes.name: List codespaces for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Lists GitHub Codespaces for the authenticated user, optionally filtering by repository ID and supporting pagination.

nodes.name: List codespaces for the organization
nodes.description: Lists active/pending GitHub Codespaces for an existing organization; admins list all, members list their own.

nodes.name: List comment changes across organization
nodes.description: Tool to list issue and PR comment changes across an organization's repositories efficiently. Use when monitoring comment activity without per-PR/per-issue polling. Filters organization events to return only comment-related events (IssueCommentEvent, PullRequestReviewCommentEvent, CommitCommentEvent). Note: Events are limited to the past 30 days and up to 300 events per timeline. Use ETag header for efficient polling to avoid rate limits.

nodes.name: List comments for a pull request review

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository owner must be known; typically from a prior tool that listed/created repos or from user context
Repository name must be known; typically from a prior tool that listed/created repos or from user context

nodes.name: List commit comments for a repository
nodes.description: Lists all commit comments for a specified repository, which must exist and be accessible.

nodes.name: List commits
nodes.description: Lists commits for a GitHub repository. Requires 'owner' (username/org) and 'repo' (repository name) parameters. Optionally filter by SHA/branch, path, author, committer, or date range.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Required repository owner identifier; typically produced by a prior tool that resolved org/user name or comes from agent context
Required repository name identifier; typically produced by a prior tool or comes from agent context

Repository name is a reference that should typically come from a prior tool that identified or created the repository, though it may be user-provided if they name the repo directly

nodes.name: Get all custom properties for an organization

The organization/owner name must be obtained from a prior tool or user provides it as a known identifier; it is a key reference to identify the organization context

nodes.name: List deliveries for an organization webhook

nodes.name: List deliveries for a repository webhook
nodes.description: Retrieves delivery attempts for a specific repository webhook to inspect its history; ensure the webhook ID exists.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Required identifier for the repository owner; typically produced by a tool listing/searching repositories or provided as a known GitHub username
Required identifier for the repository name; typically produced by a tool listing/searching repositories or provided as a known repository name

nodes.name: List deploy keys
nodes.description: Lists deploy SSH keys for a specified repository; the repository must exist.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The account owner must be known; typically comes from a prior tool that identified the GitHub account or repository, or user provides it directly as a GitHub username.
The repository name must be known; typically comes from a prior tool that identified a repository, or user provides it directly as a repo name.

nodes.name: List deployment branch policies

requiresUserInput.rationale: Account owner must be known; typically produced by repository lookup or GitHub user resolution
Repository name must be known; typically produced by repository search/list or provided as part of owner+repo pair

nodes.name: List deployments

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository owner must be specified; typically comes from a prior lookup or user context
Repository name must be specified; typically comes from a prior lookup or user context

nodes.name: List deployment statuses

requiresUserInput.rationale: GitHub repository owner must be specified; typically a user/org name that could come from a prior tool or user input, but in agent workflows often a producer_ref from earlier GitHub operations
Repository name is required and typically obtained from prior GitHub tool that lists or creates repos

nodes.name: List discussion comments

nodes.name: List discussions

Note: Team discussions are deprecated as of 2023-11-28. Consider using GitHub Discussions instead. This endpoint requires the authenticated user to be a member of the organization and have access to the team's discussions.

nodes.name: List email addresses for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Lists all email addresses for the authenticated user, including their primary status, verification status, and visibility. This action retrieves the complete list of email addresses associated with the authenticated user, providing important details such as which email is primary and whether the emails are verified.

nodes.name: List environment custom deployment rules

The 'role' field indicates the type of invitation: - 'direct_member': Regular organization member - 'admin': Organization administrator - 'billing_manager': Billing manager role - 'hiring_manager': Hiring manager role - 'reinstate': Reinstating a previous member Note: If the invitee is not a GitHub member, the 'login' field will be null and only the 'email' field will be populated.

nodes.name: List project cards (Classic - Deprecated)

For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 which uses the GraphQL API instead. See: https://github.blog/changelog/2024-05-23-sunset-notice-projects-classic/ Returns a list of project cards including: id, node_id, url, note (or null for linked cards), creator info, content_url (for issue/PR cards), column_url, project_url, timestamps, and archived status.

The new Projects does not have a REST API for listing collaborators - use the GitHub web interface to manage project collaborators instead. nodes.description: Lists repositories a GitHub App installation can access. IMPORTANT: This endpoint requires a GitHub App installation access token (ghs_* prefix) for authentication. OAuth/user access tokens (gho_*) or personal access tokens are not supported and will result in a 403 Forbidden error. Returns 'total_count' and 'repositories' array with pagination support.

nodes.name: List repositories for a user
nodes.description: Lists public repositories for a specified GitHub user, who must have an existing account.

requiresUserInput.rationale: GitHub username is a natural language identifier that the user provides directly; it's not produced by another tool in a typical workflow.

nodes.name: List repositories for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Lists repositories for the authenticated user; using 'type' with 'visibility' or 'affiliation' API parameters (not in this model) can cause a 422 error.

nodes.name: List repositories starred by a user
nodes.description: Lists repositories a valid and existing GitHub user has starred.

nodes.name: List repositories starred by the authenticated user
nodes.description: Lists repositories the authenticated user has starred, optionally sorted and paginated, including star creation timestamps via 'application/vnd.github.star+json' media type.

nodes.name: List repositories watched by a user
nodes.description: Lists repositories a given GitHub user is watching; the username must be a valid and existing GitHub user handle.

nodes.name: List repositories watched by the authenticated user
nodes.description: Lists repositories the authenticated user is watching (subscribed to for notifications). This action retrieves a list of repositories that the authenticated user is currently watching. It allows the user to manage their watched repositories effectively.

nodes.name: List repos with ghactions cache usage
nodes.description: Retrieves a paginated list of GitHub Actions cache usage statistics for repositories within an existing GitHub organization.

requiresUserInput.rationale: User must specify which GitHub organization to query for cache usage statistics; this is semantic content only a human can provide

nodes.name: List repository activities
nodes.description: Lists activities for a GitHub repository, ensuring the repository exists and is accessible.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Required parameter identifying the account owner; typically produced by repository lookup or provided by user as a known GitHub owner/org name
Required parameter identifying the repository name; typically produced by repository lookup tools

nodes.name: Get all autolinks of a repository
nodes.description: Retrieves all autolinks (e.g., for JIRA issues) configured for a repository; requires admin permissions on the repository.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository name must be provided by the user or obtained from a prior tool that lists/creates repositories; it is a concrete identifier needed to query autolinks
The owner/account username must be provided by the user or obtained from a prior tool; it is a concrete identifier paired with repo to identify the specific repository

nodes.name: List repository collaborators
nodes.description: Lists collaborators for a specified repository, provided it exists and is accessible to the authenticated user.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository owner username is a required identifier that typically comes from a prior tool that listed/searched repositories or from a user-provided repo reference that was resolved
The repository name is a required identifier that typically comes from a prior tool that listed/searched repositories or from a user-provided repo reference that was resolved

nodes.name: List repository contributors
nodes.description: Lists contributors to a specified repository, sorted by number of contributions in descending order; the repository must exist and be accessible.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository owner's username must be specified; typically comes from a prior tool that resolved or created a repository, or from user selection of a known repository owner
The repository name must be specified; typically comes from a prior tool that listed or created repositories, or from user selection of a known repository

nodes.name: List repository events
nodes.description: Lists chronological events (e.g., code pushes, issue activities, PR actions, forks) for a specified, existing GitHub repository.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository owner is a GitHub entity identifier that could come from a prior tool call or be looked up, but is required and typically produced by tools listing/searching repositories or users
