The specific protected branch name is semantic content only a user can provide. The branch must already exist with protection rules enabled, but the user specifies which branch to configure.

nodes.name: Set a repository subscription
nodes.description: Sets the authenticated user's notification subscription for a repository.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name is a required identifier that must be known or resolved from a prior tool that lists/retrieves repositories.
Repository owner is a required identifier that must be known or resolved from a prior tool that lists/retrieves repository metadata.

nodes.name: Set a thread subscription
nodes.description: Sets whether a GitHub notification thread is ignored (muted) or unignored (unmuted), for a `thread_id` that must identify an existing notification thread.

nodes.name: Set default workflow permissions for an organization
nodes.description: Updates an organization's default GITHUB_TOKEN permissions for workflows and whether GitHub Actions can approve pull requests; note that allowing Actions to approve pull requests (`can_approve_pull_request_reviews: true`) is a security risk.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The organization name is a semantic identifier provided by the user; it is not a reference produced by another tool.

nodes.name: Set default workflow permissions for a repository
nodes.description: Sets the default permissions for the GITHUB_TOKEN within a repository and configures whether GitHub Actions can approve pull requests.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The account owner of the repository is a GitHub identity that must be known or resolved; typically a downstream output from a tool that lists/fetches repositories or user info.
The repository name is a reference to a specific repository; it must be known from a prior tool call or user specification of an existing repo.

nodes.name: Set primary email visibility
nodes.description: Sets the visibility ('public' or 'private') of the authenticated user's primary email address on GitHub, if one is configured.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The visibility parameter is a semantic choice ('public' or 'private') that only the user can decide; it represents a preference, not a reference to an external entity.

nodes.name: Set GitHub Actions permissions for an organization
nodes.description: Sets the GitHub Actions permissions policy for an organization, specifying which repositories can run Actions and which actions/workflows are allowed; if 'selected' is chosen for either, manage the specific lists via other endpoints.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Organization name is semantic content that only a user can provide; it's a natural-language identifier for an org, not an ID returned by another tool.
Policy choice ('all', 'none', 'selected') is a configuration flag that a user specifies; not produced by another tool.

nodes.name: Set github actions permissions for a repository
nodes.description: Sets GitHub Actions permissions for a repository, enabling/disabling Actions and defining the policy for allowed actions and reusable workflows.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The owner is a GitHub username or organization that must be known; typically resolved from context or a prior tool that lists repositories.
The repository name is a reference that must be known; typically comes from a prior tool that lists or searches repositories.
A boolean flag indicating whether to enable or disable GitHub Actions; only a human can decide the desired state.

nodes.name: Set interaction restrictions for an organization
nodes.description: Limits interactions (comments, new issues, PRs) in an organization's public repositories by user type and duration, typically to mitigate high traffic or unwanted activity.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Semantic policy choice (which user type to restrict: existing_users, contributors_only, etc.) only a human can specify

nodes.name: Set interaction restrictions for a repository
nodes.description: Temporarily limits which GitHub users (e.g., existing_users, contributors_only) can interact (comment, open issues, create pull requests) in a repository for a specified duration.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository name must be identified; typically comes from a prior tool that lists or creates repositories, or the user names it but it should be validated/resolved.
The repository owner (username or organization) is a reference to a GitHub account that must be identified, typically from context or a prior lookup.
The interaction restriction level (e.g., 'existing_users', 'contributors_only') is a policy choice that only the user can semantically decide.

nodes.name: Set interaction restrictions for your public repositories
nodes.description: Sets or updates temporary interaction restrictions for all public repositories owned by the authenticated user, overriding any repository-specific limits.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The limit parameter specifies the type of restriction policy (e.g., 'existing_users', 'contributors_only', 'collaborators_only') which is a semantic choice only a user can determine

nodes.name: Set labels for an issue
nodes.description: Replaces all existing labels on a GitHub issue with a new set of labels.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository name must be known; typically produced by a prior tool that listed or created a repo, or looked it up by description
The repository owner must be known; typically produced by a prior tool or identified as a GitHub account
The list of label names to apply is semantic content the user provides; labels are strings representing the desired label names

nodes.name: Set custom OIDC subject claim template
nodes.description: Sets the OpenID Connect (OIDC) subject claim template for a GitHub repository, allowing use of the default template or a custom one defined by `include_claim_keys` if `use_default` is `false`.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must be provided; typically resolved from a prior tool that listed or created a repository, or user-provided as the target repo name.
Owner (username or organization) must be provided; typically resolved from a prior tool or user context, or user-provided as the target owner.
Boolean flag controlling template selection; semantic decision only a user can make.

nodes.name: Set allowed actions and workflows for an organization
nodes.description: Sets the GitHub Actions permissions for an existing organization, specifying allowed GitHub-owned actions, verified creator actions, and action/workflow patterns from public repositories.

nodes.name: Set custom labels for a self-hosted runner for an organization
nodes.description: Sets the custom labels for a self-hosted runner in an organization; this operation does not affect default system-assigned labels (e.g., 'self-hosted', 'linux', 'x64').

requiresUserInput.rationale: The organization name is typically a reference to an existing GitHub organization that must be known or looked up beforehand.
The labels array is a semantic list of custom label names that the user must provide; these are free-form strings defining what labels to assign to the runner.

nodes.name: Set repo allowed actions
nodes.description: Sets allowed GitHub Actions and reusable workflows for a repository, managing permissions for GitHub-owned, verified creator, or specific pattern-matched actions/workflows (note: `patterns_allowed` applies only to public repositories).

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name is a required identifier that must be known; typically obtained from a prior tool that lists/creates/fetches repositories.
Repository owner (account name) is a required identifier that must be known; typically obtained from a prior tool or user context.

nodes.name: Set custom labels for repo runner
nodes.description: Replaces all custom labels for a specific self-hosted runner in a repository; an empty list for `labels` removes all existing custom labels.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name is typically discovered via a prior tool that lists or searches repositories; it's a reference to an existing repo resource.
Account owner is typically discovered via a prior tool or user context; it's a reference to an existing GitHub user/org resource.
Labels are semantic content (list of label strings) that the user provides; an empty list is a valid input to remove all labels.

nodes.name: Set selected repositories for an organization variable
nodes.description: Replaces the list of repositories that can access an organization-level variable; the variable's visibility must be 'selected'.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Organization slug/name is semantic content provided by the user; not an ID produced by another tool.
Variable name is semantic content provided by the user to identify which org variable to modify.

nodes.name: Set selected repositories for an organization secret
nodes.description: Replaces the list of repositories that can access an organization secret; only effective if the secret's visibility is 'selected'. Prerequisites: - The organization secret must already exist - The secret's visibility must be set to 'selected' (not 'all' or 'private') - Requires admin:org scope or actions secrets fine-grained permission This action completely replaces the repository access list. To add or remove individual repositories without affecting others, use the dedicated add/remove repository actions instead.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The organization name is a semantic identifier that only the user can provide; it is not an ID produced by another tool.

nodes.name: Set selected repositories for a user secret
nodes.description: Defines the list of repositories permitted to access a specific Codespaces secret for the authenticated user.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The name of an existing Codespaces secret is semantic content the user must specify; there is no standard way to enumerate secrets by name, so this is user-provided.

nodes.name: Set status check contexts
nodes.description: Replaces required status check contexts for a protected branch, requiring admin permissions; an empty `contexts` array removes all checks.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must be provided by user or obtained from a prior tool that lists/creates repositories
Owner (username or organization) must be provided by user or obtained from a prior tool that identifies the account
Array of status check context names is semantic content provided by the user; represents which checks should be required

nodes.name: Set team access restrictions
nodes.description: Replaces the list of teams with push access to a protected branch. This action sets (replaces) the teams that have push access to a protected branch. Unlike 'Add team access restrictions' (POST), this action replaces the entire list rather than adding to it. Use an empty list to remove all team restrictions. Prerequisites: - The repository must be owned by an organization (not a personal account) - The branch must have protection rules enabled - The branch protection must have restrictions configured - The teams must exist in the organization and have repository access

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must be known; typically produced by a prior tool that lists or retrieves repositories, or user provides it directly as a known identifier
Organization name must be known; typically produced by prior context or user knowledge of their org

nodes.name: Set user access restrictions
nodes.description: Replaces the list of users with push access to a protected branch in an organization repository. Important notes: - This action only works on organization-owned repositories (not personal repos) - The branch must already have protection rules with restrictions enabled - This REPLACES (not adds to) the existing list of users with push access - Users must have write access to the repository to be added - The combined total of users, apps, and teams is limited to 100 items - To add users without replacing, use the 'add_user_access_restrictions' action instead

requiresUserInput.rationale: The organization owner must be specified; typically comes from a previous tool that identified or listed repositories
The repository name is typically discovered via a search or list operation, not freely provided by the user
The branch name is a semantic string that the user specifies directly (e.g., 'main', 'develop')

nodes.name: Set User Interaction Limit (GraphQL)
nodes.description: Tool to set interaction limits on a user's public repositories using GraphQL. Use when you need to temporarily restrict who can comment, open issues, or create pull requests. Limits apply to the authenticated user or a specified user.

requiresUserInput.rationale: User must specify the interaction restriction level as a semantic choice among predefined options (EXISTING_USERS, CONTRIBUTORS_ONLY, COLLABORATORS_ONLY)
Requires the GitHub global node ID of a user, which must be obtained from a prior tool that lists or fetches user data

nodes.name: Set workflow access level outside repository
nodes.description: Sets the access level for workflows outside a repository to use actions and reusable workflows within that repository.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name is a canonical identifier that typically comes from a prior tool that lists or fetches repositories, or from user knowledge of an existing repo.
Owner (username or organization) is a canonical identifier typically obtained from prior tool output or user context.
Access level is a semantic choice (none/user/organization) that only a user can meaningfully specify based on their desired security posture.

nodes.name: Star a gist
nodes.description: Stars a GitHub gist identified by `gist_id`; this action is idempotent and returns a 204 No Content status upon success, even if the gist is already starred.

nodes.name: Star a specified repository (requires owner and repo)
nodes.description: Stars a specified repository (identified by owner and repo name) for the authenticated user; this action is idempotent and succeeds even if the repository is already starred. Requires explicit repository identification via owner and repo parameters.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository name is typically obtained from a prior search/list tool (e.g., GITHUB_SEARCH_REPOSITORIES) or user navigation through repository discovery workflows. While it can be user-provided as a string, in agent flows it is normally a canonical reference from a repository lookup.

The repository owner (username) is typically resolved from a prior repository lookup or search result. Though a string, it represents a canonical entity reference in GitHub workflows rather than free-form user input.

nodes.name: Start a codespace for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Initiates the startup process for an existing GitHub Codespace (identified by `codespace_name`) if it's in a startable state like 'Available' or 'Stopped'.

nodes.name: Stop a codespace for an organization user
nodes.description: Stops a running codespace for an organization member. This action allows organization administrators to stop a codespace that belongs to a member of their organization. The codespace must be in a running state (e.g., 'Available') to be stopped. Required permissions: - OAuth/PAT: admin:org scope - Fine-grained tokens: 'Organization codespaces' (write) or 'Codespaces lifecycle admin' (write) Common HTTP responses: - 200: Codespace successfully stopped, returns codespace object - 304: Not modified (codespace already stopped) - 401: Authentication required - 403: Insufficient permissions (need admin:org) - 404: Organization, user, or codespace not found

nodes.name: Stop a codespace for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Stops a running or available Codespace for the authenticated user, pausing its execution and billing.

nodes.name: Submit a review for a pull request
nodes.description: Finalizes a pending pull request review (identified by `review_id`) with a required `event` (APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES, COMMENT) and an optional `body`.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository owner username must be known; typically obtained from a prior tool that lists or creates repositories
Repository name is required and typically comes from a prior tool that fetches or lists repositories
The review action (APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES, or COMMENT) is a semantic choice only the user can make

nodes.name: Sync a fork branch with the upstream repository
nodes.description: Synchronizes a branch in a forked GitHub repository with its upstream counterpart, assuming the repository is a fork, the branch exists, an upstream is configured, and the merge is conflict-free.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository name must be known; typically produced by a prior tool that listed or created repositories, or looked up by name. This is a concrete identifier needed for the API call.
The owner (username or organization) must be known; typically produced by a prior tool or is the authenticated user. This is a concrete identifier needed for the API call.

nodes.name: Test the push repository webhook
nodes.description: Triggers a simulated push event to test a repository's push webhook; a test event is only delivered if the webhook is subscribed to 'push' events, otherwise, it returns 204 No Content without sending a POST.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must be obtained from a prior tool that lists or creates repositories, or user must provide the exact repo name they want to test.
Repository owner (GitHub account) must be obtained from context or a prior tool that resolves ownership, or user must provide it.

nodes.name: Transfer a repository
nodes.description: Initiates a repository transfer to a new owner (who must accept the request); if the new owner is an organization, it must be configured to allow transfers.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository name must be known; typically comes from a prior tool that listed or created repos, or user provides by name requiring lookup
The current owner username/org must be known to identify which repo is being transferred; comes from prior tool output or user knowledge

nodes.name: Unblock a user
nodes.description: Unblocks a GitHub user, provided they are currently blocked by the authenticated user.

nodes.name: Unblock a user from an organization
nodes.description: Unblocks a user from an organization, allowing renewed interaction with its resources, provided the user is currently blocked (otherwise, a 404 error may occur).

nodes.name: Unfollow a user
nodes.description: Unfollows an existing GitHub user; this action is idempotent, succeeding even if the authenticated user is not currently following them.

nodes.name: Unfollow organization
nodes.description: Tool to unfollow a GitHub organization using GraphQL. Use when you need to stop following a specific organization on GitHub.

nodes.name: Unfollow user
nodes.description: Tool to unfollow a GitHub user using GraphQL. Use when you need to stop following a specific user on GitHub.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The user_id is a GraphQL Node ID that must be obtained from a prior tool call (e.g., GITHUB_GET_USER or a search tool that returns user node IDs)

nodes.name: Unlock an issue
nodes.description: Unlocks a currently locked GitHub issue in the specified repository, allowing new comments and interactions.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository owner must be specified; typically obtained from a prior tool that lists or searches repositories, or provided by user as a known GitHub account/organization name
The repository name must be specified; typically obtained from a prior tool that lists or searches repositories, or provided by user as a known repository name

nodes.name: Unlock an organization repository
nodes.description: Unlocks a repository that was locked for migration within a GitHub organization. This action is part of the organization migrations workflow. When you start a migration with `lock_repositories=true`, the source repositories are locked to prevent changes during export. After the migration is complete and applied to the target instance, use this action to unlock the repositories so they can be accessed or deleted. Requirements: - You must be an organization owner to unlock repositories - The repository must be currently locked as part of the specified migration - This endpoint does not work with GitHub App tokens or fine-grained personal access tokens Typical workflow: 1. Start an organization migration with POST /orgs/{org}/migrations 2. Wait for the migration to complete (state: "exported") 3. Apply the migration to your target instance 4. Unlock each migrated repository using this action 5. Delete the source repositories if no longer needed

nodes.name: Unlock a user repository
nodes.description: Unlocks a repository (`repo_name`) that was locked as part of a user migration (`migration_id`), making it usable or deletable; this action requires the repository to be currently locked.

nodes.name: Unstar a gist
nodes.description: Removes a star from the specified gist; the action is idempotent and will not error if the gist was not previously starred by the user.

nodes.name: Unstar a repository
nodes.description: Removes the authenticated user's star from a specified repository, which must already be starred by the user.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must be known; typically produced by a tool that lists or searches repositories, or provided by user as a known repo identifier.
Repository owner username must be known; typically produced by a tool that retrieves repository details or user information, or provided by user as a known owner identifier.

nodes.name: Update a check run
nodes.description: Updates an existing check run for a specific commit in a repository, allowing modifications to its status, conclusion, output, and other details. NOTE: This endpoint requires GitHub App authentication with 'checks:write' permission - OAuth tokens and personal access tokens cannot update check runs.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The account owner of the repository must be known; typically produced by a prior repository lookup or list operation
The repository name must be known; typically produced by a prior repository lookup or list operation

nodes.name: Update a code scanning alert
nodes.description: Updates a specific code scanning alert in a GitHub repository, primarily to change its state (e.g., 'open' or 'dismissed').

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name is typically obtained from a prior tool that lists or creates repositories, or from user context. While it could be user-provided text, in agent workflows it's usually a resolved reference.
Repository owner (GitHub account) is typically obtained from prior tool output or user context. In agent workflows, it's usually a resolved reference rather than free-form user input.
The new state ('open' or 'dismissed') is a semantic choice only a human can provide based on their intent.

nodes.name: Update a commit comment
nodes.description: Changes the body of an existing commit comment.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The new content of the comment is semantic text that only a user can provide

nodes.name: Update a custom organization role
nodes.description: Updates an existing custom role in an organization by modifying its name, description, or permissions; at least one of these fields must be provided.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Organization name is semantic content only a human can specify; it identifies which organization's role is being updated

nodes.name: Update a deployment branch policy
nodes.description: Updates the name pattern of an existing deployment branch policy for a specific environment in a repository.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository owner is a GitHub account identifier that must be known or resolved beforehand
The repository name is a specific identifier that must be known or looked up
The new name pattern is semantic content (branch matching pattern with wildcard syntax) that only a user can specify

nodes.name: Update a discussion
nodes.description: Updates the title and/or body of a specific team discussion within an organization.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Organization name is semantic content provided by the user; no lookup tool can resolve it

nodes.name: Update a discussion comment
nodes.description: Updates the body of a comment in a team's discussion within an organization.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Organization name is semantic content the user specifies; not an ID reference from another tool
The new comment body text is semantic content only a human can naturally provide
Team slug is a formatted identifier; while it could be looked up by team name, the parameter itself accepts the canonical slug format. User typically provides or knows the slug directly.

nodes.name: Update a gist
nodes.description: Updates a gist's description, and/or its files (including content, filename changes, or deletion).

nodes.name: Update a gist comment
nodes.description: Updates an existing comment on a specified gist.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The new comment text is semantic content that only a human can naturally provide; supports Markdown formatting

nodes.name: Update a label
nodes.description: Updates an existing label's name, color, or description within a specified repository.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The account owner is a semantic identifier that only the user can specify; not produced by another tool in typical workflows
The repository name is a semantic identifier that only the user can specify; while repos can be listed, the agent typically receives this from user intent

nodes.name: Update a milestone
nodes.description: Updates a milestone in a repository, identified by `owner`, `repo`, and `milestone_number`, by allowing modification of its `title`, `state`, `description`, or `due_on`; at least one of these four attributes must be provided to perform an update.

nodes.name: Update an environment variable
nodes.description: Updates an existing environment variable's name and/or value in a specific GitHub repository environment; requires providing either a new name or a new value.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The current name of the environment variable is semantic content that only the user knows; it is not an ID produced by another tool.
Repository name is typically resolved from a list/get operation or provided as a reference from a prior tool that created or queried repositories.
Repository owner is typically resolved from a prior context or tool operation that identified the owning account.
The environment name is semantic content that the user provides; it must be URL-encoded by the user or agent before passing to this tool.

nodes.name: Update an issue
nodes.description: Updates an existing GitHub issue's title, body, state, milestone, labels, or assignees; `state_reason` is only processed if `state` also changes, and use `null` or `[]` to clear applicable fields.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository owner must be provided or obtained from a prior tool that listed/created a repository
Repository name must be provided or obtained from a prior tool that listed/created a repository

nodes.name: Update an issue comment
nodes.description: Updates an existing comment on an issue or pull request within a specified repository. You can only update comments that the authenticated user has permission to edit. The comment_id can be obtained from the list_issue_comments or get_an_issue_comment actions.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The new comment content is semantic text only a human can naturally compose
Repository name must be known; typically comes from a prior tool that listed/created repos or from user specifying it as a known reference
Repository owner username must be known; typically comes from prior context or user specifying the account

nodes.name: Update an organization
nodes.description: Updates an organization's settings; changing security-related fields requires admin, owner, or security manager permissions.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The organization identifier is a required reference to an existing organization that must be known or retrieved from a prior tool

nodes.name: Update an organization variable
nodes.description: Updates an existing GitHub Actions organization variable's name, value, or visibility (`all`, `private`, `selected`), requiring `selected_repository_ids` with valid repository IDs if visibility is `selected`.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Organization name is a semantic identifier provided by the user; not a reference to another tool's output
Current variable name is a semantic identifier that the user must know and specify; case-sensitive identifier

nodes.name: Update an organization webhook
nodes.description: Updates the configuration (URL, content type, secret, SSL verification), subscribed events, active status, or name of an existing webhook for a specified organization.

nodes.name: Update a project
nodes.description: Updates an existing GitHub project (V2) attributes like title, description, visibility, and state. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects V2 GraphQL API. The legacy Projects (classic) REST API was sunset on April 1, 2025. The project is identified by its number (visible in the project URL) and owner (either a username or organization name). If no owner is specified, the authenticated user is assumed. At least one field to update must be provided.

nodes.name: Update a pull request
nodes.description: Updates an existing pull request, allowing changes to attributes like title, body, state, base branch, and maintainer modification settings.

requiresUserInput.rationale: GitHub repository owner must be known; typically from a prior tool that listed/retrieved repos or from user specification of an owner account
GitHub repository name must be known; typically from a prior tool that listed/retrieved repos or user specification

nodes.name: Update a pull request branch
nodes.description: Updates an open pull request's head branch by merging the latest changes from its base branch, if mergeable and repository merging is enabled; operates asynchronously.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must be provided or obtained from a prior tool that lists/creates repos
Repository owner (account) must be known; typically from user context or a prior GitHub tool result

nodes.name: Update a reference
nodes.description: Updates an existing Git reference (e.g., a branch or tag) to point to a specific commit SHA. By default, only allows fast-forward updates (where the new commit is a descendant of the current one). Set `force=true` to allow non-fast-forward updates that rewrite history. IMPORTANT: The reference must already exist - use 'Create a reference' action to create new refs.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository owner must be identified; typically comes from a prior GitHub lookup or list operation, or user provides it directly
Repository name must be identified; typically comes from a prior GitHub lookup or list operation, or user provides it directly

nodes.name: Update a release
nodes.description: Updates an existing release in a GitHub repository, allowing modification of its attributes; if linking a discussion, the `discussion_category_name` must refer to an existing category in the repository.

requiresUserInput.rationale: GitHub repository owner; typically comes from a prior tool that lists/creates repos or from user lookup of org/user name
Repository name; paired with owner as a canonical reference; typically produced by a repo listing or creation tool

nodes.name: Update a release asset
nodes.description: Updates the name, label, or state of a release asset in a GitHub repository, requiring at least one of these properties to be provided for modification.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository owner is a GitHub account identifier that must be known; typically produced by a prior tool that fetched/listed repositories or provided by user knowledge of the org/user name, but semantically it is a reference to an entity
The repository name must be known before this tool can execute; typically produced by a prior tool that listed/fetched repositories

nodes.name: Update a repository
nodes.description: Updates settings for an existing GitHub repository, such as name, description, visibility, merge strategies, and security configurations.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Must be an actual existing repository name; typically obtained from a prior tool that lists or creates repositories
Must be an actual existing GitHub username or organization; typically obtained from a prior tool or authenticated context

nodes.name: Update a repository invitation
nodes.description: Updates an active repository invitation to modify the invited user's permissions; the specified repository and invitation must exist.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The repository owner (username or organization) must be known; typically provided by user or resolved from context, but treated as a semantic reference that identifies a GitHub account
The repository name must be known; typically provided by user or resolved from a prior search/list operation

nodes.name: Update a repository ruleset
nodes.description: Updates an existing repository ruleset, identified by `ruleset_id` for a given repository, allowing partial updates to its configuration such as name, target, enforcement, bypass actors, conditions, and rules.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The account owner must be known; typically produced by a repository lookup or provided as a known GitHub organization/user
The repository name must be known; typically produced by a repository lookup or list tool

nodes.name: Update a repository variable
nodes.description: Updates the name and/or value of an existing GitHub Actions variable in a repository.

requiresUserInput.rationale: GitHub account owner must be provided; typically comes from a repository lookup or user context
Repository name is a required reference; typically obtained from a repository lookup or listing

nodes.name: Update a repository webhook
nodes.description: Updates the URL, content type, secret, SSL verification, events, or active status for an existing repository webhook, specified by `owner`, `repo`, and `hook_id`.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must be known; typically obtained from a prior tool listing repos or from user specification of a repo already identified
Repository owner (username or org) must be known; typically from a prior operation or user context

nodes.name: Update a team
nodes.description: Updates a team's settings (e.g., name, description, privacy, parent team) within a GitHub organization, identified by its slug and organization name.

nodes.name: Update branch protection
nodes.description: Updates the protection settings for an existing branch in a repository, which must not contain wildcard characters.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must come from a prior tool that listed or created repositories, or be a well-known identifier the user provides.
Owner/account name must come from a prior tool or be a well-known GitHub account identifier.
Branch name is semantic content specifying which branch to protect; typically user-provided or from prior branch listing.
User logins must be known GitHub user identifiers.
User logins allowed to bypass PR requirements must be known GitHub user identifiers.
User logins allowed to dismiss reviews must be known GitHub user identifiers.

nodes.name: Update a code scanning default setup configuration
nodes.description: Updates the default setup configuration for code scanning in a repository; GitHub Advanced Security must be enabled for the repository.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name must be provided; typically comes from a prior tool that listed or created repos, or user must specify it
Account owner must be provided; typically comes from a prior tool or user must specify it

nodes.name: Update a codespace for the authenticated user
nodes.description: Updates an existing GitHub Codespace's machine type, display name, or recent folders for the authenticated user; machine type changes take effect on the next start.

nodes.name: Update Git LFS Preference
nodes.description: Sets the Git Large File Storage (LFS) preference for a repository during a source import. NOTE: This endpoint has been deprecated by GitHub as of April 12, 2024, and is no longer available. The Source Imports REST API has been retired due to very low usage levels. Please use the GitHub Importer tool at https://github.com/new/import for repository imports.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name is a reference to a specific repository that must be identified beforehand, typically from a prior tool that lists or creates repositories.
Owner (username or organization) is a reference identifier that must be known or obtained from context, typically from prior tool outputs or user account context.
This is a semantic choice between two mutually exclusive options (opt_in or opt_out) that only the user can decide based on their import requirements.

nodes.name: Update issue type
nodes.description: Update an existing issue type for a GitHub organization. Requires the authenticated user to be an organization administrator. OAuth tokens need admin:org scope.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The name of the issue type is semantic content provided by the user
Boolean flag indicating whether to enable/disable the issue type; a configuration choice made by the user

nodes.name: Update a webhook configuration for an organization
nodes.description: Updates the configuration (URL, content type, secret, SSL verification) for an existing webhook within a specified organization.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Organization name is a semantic identifier that the user must provide; it is not an ID produced by another tool

nodes.name: Update an existing project card (Classic - Deprecated)
nodes.description: Updates an existing project card's note and/or archived status (GitHub Classic Projects). DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Projects (classic) and its REST API were sunset on April 1, 2025. This action will return 404 for most requests on GitHub.com. However, it may still work on GitHub Enterprise Server instances where classic projects are enabled. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 which uses the GraphQL API instead. See: https://github.blog/changelog/2024-05-23-sunset-notice-projects-classic/

nodes.name: Update an existing project column (Classic - Deprecated)
nodes.description: Updates the name of an existing column, identified by `column_id`, in a GitHub project (classic). DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Classic Projects (V1) and its REST API were sunset on April 1, 2025. This action will return a 404 error on GitHub.com. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 which uses the GraphQL API instead. See: https://github.blog/changelog/2024-05-23-sunset-notice-projects-classic/

requiresUserInput.rationale: The new name is semantic content only a human can provide

nodes.name: Update a review for a pull request
nodes.description: Updates the body text of an existing pull request review.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The new review body text is semantic content only a human can compose
Repository name must be obtained from a tool that lists/gets repositories or from explicit user knowledge of the target repo
Repository owner username must be obtained from a tool or user knowledge of the account

nodes.name: Update a review comment for a pull request
nodes.description: Updates the body of an existing review comment on a pull request.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The new comment text is semantic content only a human can naturally provide
Repository name is typically produced by a GitHub search/list tool or must be known from prior context
Repository owner (username or org) is typically produced by a GitHub search/list tool or must be known from prior context

nodes.name: Update pull request review protection
nodes.description: Updates pull request review protection settings (e.g., required approvals, review dismissal, bypass allowances) for a branch; branch protection features must be available for the repository.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository owner must be identified; typically a GitHub organization or user account. Can come from a prior tool that listed repositories or from user providing org/user login.
Repository name must be identified; typically comes from a tool that listed or retrieved repositories, or user specifies it by name.

nodes.name: Update repository preferences for check suites
nodes.description: Updates repository preferences for automatic Check Suite creation on code pushes, allowing configuration for specific GitHub Apps that must be installed on the repository with `checks:write` permission.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The account owner (username or organization) is semantic content that only the user can identify based on context
The repository name is semantic content that only the user can identify; while it could theoretically be produced by a list tool, it is typically provided directly by the user

nodes.name: Update a webhook configuration for a repository
nodes.description: Updates the configuration (e.g., payload URL, content type, secret, SSL verification) for an existing webhook in a specified repository.

requiresUserInput.rationale: GitHub repository owner must be identified; typically comes from a prior tool that retrieved or listed repositories
GitHub repository name must be identified; typically comes from a prior tool that retrieved or listed repositories

nodes.name: Update resource access with tokens
nodes.description: Revokes organization access for the personal access tokens identified by `pat_ids`; this action must be performed by a GitHub App, and `pat_ids` must be valid and associated with the organization. AUTHENTICATION REQUIREMENT: This endpoint requires GitHub App authentication with 'Personal access tokens' organization permissions (write). Standard personal access tokens CANNOT use this endpoint. The GitHub App must also have access to the organization.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Organization name is semantic content provided by the user; case-insensitive string identifier.
The operation type (e.g., 'revoke') is a semantic action choice provided by the user.

nodes.name: Update status check protection
nodes.description: Updates required status checks for a branch, optionally requiring it to be up-to-date before merging.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Repository name is typically obtained from a prior tool that lists or creates repositories, or from user context in a repository-scoped workflow
Repository owner (username or organization) is typically obtained from prior context or a repository lookup tool

nodes.name: Update Subscription
nodes.description: Tool to update notification subscription state for issues, pull requests, or discussions. Use when you need to subscribe, unsubscribe, or ignore notifications for a specific GitHub item.

requiresUserInput.rationale: User must specify desired subscription state as semantic preference (SUBSCRIBED/UNSUBSCRIBED/IGNORED)

nodes.name: Update the authenticated user
nodes.description: Updates the authenticated user's GitHub profile; a new public email must be verified, and existing private emails remain private even if specified.

nodes.name: Revoke Token Organization Access
nodes.description: Revokes a fine-grained personal access token's access to an organization. IMPORTANT: This endpoint can ONLY be called by GitHub Apps with 'Personal access tokens' organization permissions (write). Personal access tokens (PATs) cannot use this endpoint. Use GITHUB_LIST_ORG_RESOURCE_ACCESS_TOKENS to get valid pat_id values for tokens that have access to your organization.

requiresUserInput.rationale: Organization name is semantic content provided by the user; no tool produces this.

nodes.name: Update User List
nodes.description: Tool to update an existing GitHub user list. Use when you need to modify the name, description, or privacy settings of a user list.

nodes.name: Update user lists for item
nodes.description: Tool to update which of the viewer's lists an item belongs to on GitHub. Use when you need to add an item (repository, issue, pull request, etc.) to specific user lists or move it between lists.

nodes.name: Update Project V2 Item for User
nodes.description: Tool to update a field value for an item in a user's GitHub ProjectV2. Use when you need to modify a field value of an existing project item.

requiresUserInput.rationale: The value to set is semantic content provided by the user, formatted according to field type (text, number, single select option ID, date, iteration ID)

nodes.name: Change User Status
nodes.description: Tool to update your status on GitHub. Use when you need to set or clear your GitHub profile status with an emoji, message, and availability indicator.

nodes.name: Upload a release asset
nodes.description: Tool to upload a binary asset to an existing GitHub release. Use when you need to attach files like installers or backups to a release programmatically.

requiresUserInput.rationale: GitHub account owner must come from a prior tool that lists/identifies repositories or releases, or be user-provided as a known account name

nodes.name: Upload an analysis as sarif data

requiresUserInput.rationale: Git reference formatted as refs/heads/<branch>, refs/tags/<tag>, refs/pull/<number>/merge, or refs/pull/<number>/head; must be provided by user or workflow context
Repository name must be known; typically comes from a prior lookup or GitHub context tool
